column3: A scientific or technical observation or examination of a physical thing in which one or more properties are investigated to gain knowledge. Covers the full range of scientific examination from visual inspection through to instrumental analysis. May produce simple recorded findings, complex data outputs, or images. S27 is the superclass of S21: Measurement (E16); quantitative measurement events should use the dedicated Measurement model.

This model records a scientific or technical examination event. It captures identification, description, event typing, responsible actors, time and place, the entity observed, the property type examined, methods and protocols followed, equipment employed, and the outputs produced. Outputs may include images (EX_Digital_Image), digital data objects such as spectra or datasets (D1: Digital Object), and documentary records (E73: Observation Record). Interpretation of results and knowledge creation derived from outputs are out of scope here.
The entity or entities this observation relates to. Can be a heritage object, sample, examination event, or any other CRM entity.
The actor (individual or organisation) responsible for carrying out or authorising the observation.

column1: //links E39: Institution or Person --> person, organisation [confirmed]

column3: The place where the observation occurred (e.g., laboratory, examination room, imaging suite).
Documents that describe or evidence the observation (e.g., examination reports, condition reports, analytical notes).

A defined method or protocol followed during the observation or examination activity, referencing relevant techniques and where required this document would describe the use and setup of any specific tools or equipment. (e.g., a standard examination procedure or analytical protocol).
A digital data output produced by the observation event,  such as a images, spectrum, elemental map, or dataset.
This optional field to link to a definition of a specific area/place/feature on an the observed thing (Heritage Object, Sample, etc).

column3: The overall motivation for carrying out the Observation. This describes the research, conservation, or technical question being addressed.
A controlled type/category for the observation (e.g., visual examination, optical microscopy, X-ray fluorescence, infrared reflectography, UV fluorescence, SEM-EDX, Raman spectroscopy).
The type of property being investigated (e.g., colour, fluorescence response, surface morphology, elemental composition, layer structure, molecular composition).
The timespan during which the observation occurred.

A textual or structured documentary record produced by the observation event, such as examination notes, an analytical summary, or a condition observation report.
